channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Venice protesters rally against US ambassador’s superyacht tour
item.description: Protesters clashed with police in Venice outside US Ambassador Tilman Fertitta’s superyacht.

item.title: In New York’s ‘Little Palestine’, fans cheer for Spain in World Cup final
item.description: Tournament has showcased unity and solidarity in Brooklyn's sizeable Arab American community, football fans say.

item.title: Five football players who rose to social media stardom at the World Cup
item.description: On-pitch heroics and loveable personalities have skyrocketed the social media following of some footballers.

item.title: Iranian official says the policy of negotiating during war is over
item.description: Mohsen Rezaee has warned that if the US continues bombing Iran, Tehran will escalate its response into full-scale war.

item.title: Israeli army destroys three schools in southern Lebanon, minister says
item.description: At least 20 schools have now been completely destroyed in southern Lebanon, with 100 more damaged.

item.title: Targets of latest US strikes on Iran signal strategic shift
item.description: The US has broadened its military campaign in Iran, says Al Jazeera’s Mike Hanna.

item.title: Analyst questions Trump’s renewed focus on US election integrity
item.description: Eric Ham argues President Donald Trump’s renewed focus on election security is attempt to expand federal authority.

item.title: Landslide in southwest China kills eight and leaves 34 missing
item.description: A landslide on the outskirts of the southwestern Chinese city of Chongqing has killed at least eight and left 34 missing

item.title: Trump Media reportedly mulling fee for first access to social media posts
item.description: US president has frequently been accused of trading government influence for improper financial gain.

item.title: UK by-election sees record 34 candidates as Farage seeks return
item.description: The Reform UK leader seeks to regain his seat after resigning amid gifts controversy.

item.title: Homeland Security Secretary Mullin reiterates Trump threats about elections
item.description: Mullin pledged to make Trump's election security demands 'mandatory' and implement a 'maximum pressure' campaign.

item.title: Iran war live: US and Iran exchange deadly attacks for 7th night
item.description: Intense fighting between the two sides as US attacks shift further into Iran’s interior.

item.title: EU urges Israel to halt settlement expansion as settlers attack children
item.description: Pressure is growing within the EU to sanction Israel for its illegal settlements and violence against Palestinians.

item.title: Trump threatens Canada with steeper tariff costs over wildfire smoke
item.description: US president has frequently pushed false claims blaming the mismanagement of rival politicians for large wildfires.

item.title: Iraq signs deals with Western oil firms, including to revive Syria pipeline
item.description: Deal with Chevron aims to rebuild a crude oil pipeline that runs from Iraq's oil-rich Kirkuk to Syria's Baniyas.

item.title: US launches seventh straight night of strikes on Iran
item.description: The US military says it's degrading Iran's military but has been accused by Tehran of targeting civilian infrastructure.

item.title: Tackling Messi ‘huge challenge’ for Spain in World Cup final: Merino
item.description: Spanish midfielder says he has 'huge confidence' in his abilities after his impressive performances as a substitute.

item.title: Herbert leads British Open golf, equals record lowest score at a major
item.description: Australian golfer Herbert ended the second round of the British Open with a two-shot lead on eight-under.

item.title: Star speedcubers square off at European championship
item.description: More than 1,200 speedcubers from 53 countries are descending are attending the European Speedcubing Championship.

item.title: ICE agent behind fatal shooting in Maine reportedly had troubled history
item.description: Relatives of David Brouillette, the agent who shot and killed a man in Maine, said he had record of violent behaviour.

item.title: Food emergency deepens across Uganda’s Karamoja
item.description: Food emergency deepens across Uganda’s Karamoja

item.title: World Cup winners to receive US-style championship rings, FIFA says
item.description: Players' rings will be limited edition of 2,026 individually numbered pieces, with the rest available for public sale.

item.title: Houthi supporters rally in Sanaa against ‘Saudi-led blockade’
item.description: Thousands of Houthi supporters rallied in Sanaa against what they described as the Saudi-led blockade against Yemen.

item.title: Suspected Somali pirates seize tanker near Yemen amid wave of hijackings
item.description: The seizure of the Asana is the latest in a rapidly growing wave of piracy sweeping the Gulf of Aden.

item.title: Top IRS lawyer to resign post amid disagreements with Trump administration
item.description: Reports indicate a controversial settlement deal and disputes over tax audits may have prompted Ken Kies to step down.
